summary: First pedestrian fatality involving an automated test vehicle. NTSB found the automated system did not classify the pedestrian in time and the safety operator was inattentive.
A Cruise robotaxi dragged a pedestrian who had first been struck by a human-driven car. California DMV suspended Cruise driverless permits days later.
Waymo recalled 672 vehicles after one struck a telephone pole in Phoenix during a pullover maneuver. No injuries; software update deployed fleet-wide.
